mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations through a higher discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor income and falling corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than half of the triggered episodes where UNRATE rises more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a synchronized contraction in demand and output that amplifies downside risks to corporate profits, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and INDPRO fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of concurrent occurrences, or the interaction term does not significantly improve prediction over individual series (F-test p greater than 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days only when the federal funds rate is below 2.0 percent, because accommodative financing amplifies the demand recovery signal.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of instances when PAYEMS increases while FEDFUNDS is below 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and revenue, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of weeks when INDPRO rises month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,300 thousand units indicates a collapsing residential construction sector and negative household wealth effects, dragging S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months when HOUST is below 1300 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the unemployment rate and decreases in payroll employment define a broad labor market deterioration that forecasts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days more reliably than either series alone.
failure_condition: The simultaneous signal of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of joint occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant at the 5 percent level.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days only when consumer price inflation is subdued, because high inflation erodes real purchasing power and corporate margins.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns during low-inflation regimes, producing a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
